clusters.headline: South Africa: Villagers Build Own Bridge to Reach Clinic - South African News Briefs - July 13, 2026
South Africa says more than 53,000 foreigners deported in migration campaign
clusters.summary: South Africa is cracking down on undocumented migrants following widespread anti-immigration protests.

clusters.headline: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran and Tehran attacks U.S. allies in the Gulf
clusters.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.

clusters.headline: How former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani built Qatar’s economy
clusters.summary: Sheikh Hamad, who passed away aged 74 on Sunday, reshaped Qatar's economy by building lasting wealth and growth.

clusters.headline: World Cup 2026: Muslim footballers challenge Europe's identity debate
Sam Neill, Jurassic Park actor, dies 'unexpectedly' at 78
clusters.summary: New Zealand actor Sam Neill achieved international fame playing Dr. Alan Grant in the 1993 film Jurassic Park. But his career included many other notable TV and film appearances.

clusters.headline: East Africa: Somalia, Egypt FMs Discuss Bilateral Ties, Horn of Africa, Reject Recognition of Somaliland
clusters.summary: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somalia's Foreign Minister Abdisalam Ali Dhaay held a telephone conversation with his Egyptian counterpart, Badr Abdelatty, to discuss bilateral relations and regional developments, with a particular focus on the Horn of Africa, Somalia's foreign ministry said on Sunday.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Court Restricts Lawyers in Police Force but Not Appointed As Legal Officers to Only Prosecution
clusters.summary: [Premium Times] The judge also ordered the police force, Police Service Commission (PSC) and the Inspector-General of the Federation to deploy "police officers who are legal practitioners to every Police Division in Nigeria to assist with the enforcement of human rights in policing at the Divisions.

clusters.headline: Anthony Zurcher: From Trump critic to ally, Lindsey Graham was a political survivor of the Maga era
clusters.summary: The senator's career was marked by a willingness to adapt to America's dramatically changing political climate, writes the BBC's North America correspondent.

clusters.headline: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
clusters.summary: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Nigeria Designed the Cure for Its Electricity Crisis. It Stopped Administering It Halfway.
clusters.summary: [African Arguments] Ask anyone who has lived in Nigeria what NEPA stands for, and they will tell you it stands for Never Expect Power Always. clusters.headline: ‘Extraordinary confluence’: What China’s latest military moves reveal about its broader strategy
clusters.summary: From joint air patrols with Russia and coast guard activity east of Taiwan to a rare missile test in the Pacific, China’s recent moves - although not necessarily coordinated - reflect a push to make a wider range of operations increasingly routine, analysts say.
